title: Fetch - per-host politeness: rate limit + robots.txt
description:
Session 3 (Fetch) chain. Depends on the static fetcher (GH-26, `scholarship_factory/fetch.py`: `fetch_url`, `FetchResult`) - MERGED. Adds the good-citizen layer: one rate limiter **per host** + respect for `robots.txt`. Cheap insurance against IP bans (locked default from the S3 design).

## Locked decisions (honor)

- **Per-host min-interval rate limiting** (default ~2s between requests to the same host, configurable). Different hosts do not block each other.
- **Respect robots.txt**: before fetching a URL, consult the host's robots.txt (fetched once per host per run, cached in memory; use stdlib `urllib.robotparser` semantics for our User-Agent falling back to `*`). A disallowed URL is **not fetched** and yields an honest `FetchResult` (`ok` False, `error` naming robots as the reason) - never an exception.
- robots.txt itself unreachable/malformed -> **fail open** (treat as allowed); politeness is insurance, not a gate that can wedge the whole run.
- No global queue/scheduler - this wraps single fetches; v1 runs are sequential.

## Scope

1. A `PoliteFetcher` (or equivalent thin layer) over `fetch_url`: same signature/return, adding per-host spacing + robots check. Sleep/clock injectable for tests (no real sleeping in the suite).
2. In-memory per-host state (last-request time, parsed robots); no persistence.
3. Wire nothing else - callers opt in by using the polite layer (the pipeline ticket composes it).
acceptance_criteria: Two consecutive fetches to the same host are spaced by the configured interval (asserted via injected clock/sleep - no real sleep in tests); fetches to different hosts are not delayed.
A URL disallowed by the host's robots.txt is not fetched (underlying transport never called) and returns a `FetchResult` with `ok` False and a robots-specific `error`.
robots.txt returning 404/timeout -> the fetch proceeds (fail open).
robots.txt is requested at most once per host per fetcher instance.
All tests offline (`httpx.MockTransport` / injected fakes); `uv run pytest -q` green.
